title: Resume Architecture, Evidence Statements, and Machine-Robust Delivery · Hiring Decision Lens: Resume Priority
summary: A resume uses decorative columns, keyword blocks, vague adjectives, unsupported impact numbers, and identical content for every role. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resume priority increment in truthful role-targeted resume system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.
Provide keyboard-operable, reflowing, structured, concise, low-motion, text-equivalent case evidence with a usable offline alternative.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Resume Priority · Theory Resume Priority
steps.instruction:
theory case — A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The theory variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Resume Priority contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Resume Priority · Theory Resume Priority.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review. For Predict Resume Priority · Theory Resume Priority, keep Resume Priority connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The theory variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Resume Priority condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing

options.text: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.
A resume must preserve the full chronology with equal detail. That claim fails the predict resume priority evidence boundary for Resume Priority.

For predict resume priority, apply Resume Priority without its stated preconditions, omit the truthful role-targeted resume system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Resume Priority for truthful role-targeted resume system.

content.text: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, changed candidate, rejected failure, causal repair, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer limit; then calibrate the application or career decision to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Resume Priority while producing truthful role-targeted resume system.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A two-column resume contains vague adjectives, hidden text, inconsistent dates, unsupported percentages, broken links, and a PDF with scrambled extraction order. The worked example variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review. A defensible solution applies Resume Priority, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to truthful role-targeted resume system.

content.code:
# Worked focus: career-resume-priority — Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-2
focus: career-resume-priority
decision-evidence: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A resume must preserve the full chronology with equal detail. This fails the Resume Priority contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: GitHub Profile Documentation (GitHub Docs current 2026-07-15) — Controls profile visibility, optional public identity, pinned work, contribution context, and the personal safety consequences of public disclosure.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Prioritize the candidate value proposition and strongest role-relevant evidence for a rapid human review.
